subject: [PATCH v2 08/10] nfsd: split nfsd4_copy into transient and durable async copy objects

2026-07-09.patch_summary: This patch splits the nfsd4_copy object into two separate objects: a transient one used in the request buffer and a durable one that outlives the compound operation. The durable object, named nfsd4_async_copy, contains the necessary fields for asynchronous copy operations, such as worker kthread, reaper linkage, and callback functions. This separation is intended to fix lifetime bugs related to the dual identity of the original nfsd4_copy object. No behavioral changes are introduced in this patch, which is a step towards integrating copy stateids into the common nfs4_stid infrastructure.
